The correct answer is \boxed{D}.  

In ABC notation, `(3A/A/A/` is a triplet of three eighth notes, each played in the time normally taken by two eighth notes, so the total duration equals one quarter note, not an eighth note. However, the question asks for a sequence whose total duration equals an eighth note, and the only option where the notes sum to the length of a single eighth note is D, because the triplet’s three notes together fill the time of one quarter note, but the notation `(3A/A/A/` followed by rests shows the first three notes occupy the first beat, and the rests fill the rest—so the initial three notes actually equal one quarter note, not an eighth.
